2605.22168.title: Measuring Cross-Modal Synergy: A Benchmark for VLM Explainability
2605.22168.paper_summary: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) map complex visual inputs to semantic spaces, but interpreting the cross-modal reasoning of VLMs currently relies on post-hoc explainers evaluated via unimodal perturbation metrics. We expose a limitation in this paradigm: because multimodal datasets contain language priors and modality biases, VLMs frequently exhibit cross-modal redundancy, allowing them to answer visual queries using text alone. Consequently, unimodal metrics penalize faithful explainers, triggering an evaluation collapse where visual and textual rankings fundamentally contradict each other. %(Kendall's $τ= -0.06$). To resolve this, we introduce Synergistic Faithfulness ($\mathcal{F}_{syn}$), a scalable metric rooted in the Shapley Interaction Index that strictly isolates the joint Harsanyi dividend between modalities, serving as a highly accurate surrogate ($ρ= 0.92$) while achieving a $24\times$ computational speedup. Evaluating 8 distinct XAI methods across 3 VLM architectures and 3 benchmark datasets, reveals that explainers proposed for VLMs heavily over-index on visual salience and significantly underperform adapted attention-based methods in capturing true cross-modal synergy. By decoupling visual plausibility from cross-modal faithfulness, this work provides a rigorous evaluation framework required to safely audit VLM reasoning in high-stakes deployments.
2605.22168.paper_summary_zh: 視覺-語言模型（VLMs）將複雜的視覺輸入映射到語義空間，但目前解釋 VLMs 的跨模態推理依賴於通過單模擾動指標評估的事後解釋器。我們揭示了這一範式的限制：由於多模態數據集包含語言先驗和模態偏見，VLMs 經常表現出跨模態冗餘，使它們能夠僅使用文本回答視覺查詢。因此，單模指標懲罰忠實的解釋器，觸發評估崩潰，導致視覺和文本排名根本上相互矛盾。%(Kendall's $τ= -0.06$)。為了解決這個問題，我們引入了協同忠實性（$\mathcal{F}_{syn}$），這是一種基於 Shapley 互動指數的可擴展指標，嚴格隔離模態之間的聯合 Harsanyi 股息，作為一個高度準確的替代指標（$ρ= 0.92$），同時實現 $24\times$ 的計算加速。在 3 種 VLM 架構和 3 個基準數據集上評估 8 種不同的 XAI 方法，顯示為 VLMs 提出的解釋器在視覺顯著性上過度指標化，並在捕捉真正的跨模態協同方面顯著表現不佳，遠低於適應的基於注意力的方法。通過將視覺合理性與跨模態忠實性解耦，這項工作提供了一個嚴謹的評估框架，必要時可安全地審核 VLM 推理在高風險部署中的表現。
2605.22168.author: Joël Roman Ky et.al.
2605.22168.authors: Joël Roman Ky, Salah Ghamizi, Maxime Cordy
